name: Decorated Spice Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon each ground nutmeg and salt
1/4 teaspoon each ground allspice and cloves
1 1/2 cups butter or margarine, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix flour, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, salt, allspice, and cloves until blended.
2. In another large bowl, beat butter and sugar with an electric mixer until light and fluffy. Beat in egg and vanilla.
3. Reduce mixer speed to low and gradually add the flour mixture, beating until well blended.
4. Divide dough into thirds. Shape each into a ball and flatten to a 1-inch-thick round. Wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 1 hour or until firm enough to roll.
5. Preheat oven to 375°F. Have cookie sheets ready.
6. On a lightly floured surface, roll one third of the dough at a time (keep the remainder refrigerated) to 1/4-inch thickness. Cut dough into desired shapes with floured 3-inch cookie cutters.
7. Place cookies about 1 inch apart on ungreased cookie sheets. Chill and reroll scraps as needed.
8. Bake for about 12 minutes or until cookies are golden on undersides and edges are just starting to brown. Cool completely on the sheet on a wire rack.
9. Ice and decorate as desired. Let stand until icing hardens, about 2 hours.
